payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: PRD addresses all five intent requirements with traceable promise contracts
Strict gate semantics (real revise/deny blocks, contract retry separate, missing verdict non-accepting, fallback only after exhaustion) are preserved in the PRD
Out-of-scope explicitly forbids auto-accepting/hiding missing Cursor verdicts and changing reviewer_unavailable_policy semantics

criteria: All five intent requirements have a corresponding promise contract: met
Out-of-scope protects gate semantics and forbids auto-accept: met
Grill findings address principal risks and are resolved: met
Artifact sha256 confirmed against handoff: not met (approval blocked)
Retry budget proven sufficient for diagnosed cluster: not met (deferred to tuning/TDD)
confidence.rationale: PRD content fully reviewed and maps cleanly to every intent requirement with well-formed promise contracts and resolved grill findings; confidence held below 0.9 because artifact hash integrity was not cryptographically confirmed (approval blocked) and retry-budget efficacy against the observed 7-failure cluster is asserted-by-configurability rather than demonstrated.

payload.content: PRD for bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retry hardening covers all five intent requirements via explicit promise contracts P1-P5 (retry-before-fallback, exhausted-retries-stay-typed-unavailable, contract-retry-separate, policy-threaded-through-workflow, real-revise/deny-still-blocks). Out-of-scope is protective; four grill findings resolved. ACCEPT with three non-blocking objections carried to TDD/impl gates.

assumptions_to_verify: That watchdog-timeout retries (Impl Decision) are safe and intended despite P1-P3 framing only SDK exceptions
That a configurable budget with sane defaults is an acceptable PRD-stage answer to efficacy rather than a hard-coded proven budget
That on-disk artifacts are the same bytes referenced by the handoff sha256
contradictions_checked: Whether retry path could downgrade a real revise/deny: PRD P5 + grill Finding 1 explicitly preserve AND-gate, no contradiction
Whether fallback could run before exhaustion: P1/P2 + grill Finding 3 gate fallback on exhaustion, no contradiction
Whether contract retry and infra retry share budget: P3 keeps them separate, no contradiction
Whether missing verdict could count as accept: P2/P5 and Out-of-Scope forbid it, no contradiction
missing_evidence: Cryptographic confirmation that on-disk prd.md/issues.md/grill-findings.md match the handoff sha256 values
Timing data on the June 1 cluster (failure spacing) to justify a default retry_limit/backoff
Explicit default values for retry_limit and backoff and the backoff curve shape
critical_review.strongest_objection: The PRD never demonstrates that any bounded retry budget would have actually absorbed the diagnosed failure, which was seven consecutive Cursor attempts failing in one cluster before a later success; a small default budget with backoff could still exhaust against a multi-second cluster, leaving the operator-interruption problem unsolved despite the new code.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A genuine intent requirement left uncovered by the promise contracts, evidence the PRD weakens a strict gate semantic (e.g. allows retry to mask a real revise/deny or run fallback pre-exhaustion), or proof the on-disk PRD diverges from the handoff sha256 — any of these would move the decision to revise or deny.

payload.content: Re-review of revised planning artifacts. Prior block was deterministic planning_validation (ISS-001 0 slices, GRILL-001 invalid finding statuses). Both failing checks are now satisfied and slices cohere with the working-tree implementation. ACCEPT.

supervisor re-runs the deterministic validator and will now pass ISS-001/GRILL-001

contradictions_checked: manifest workspace_snapshot issues hash 8590af8 (blocked) vs handoff be05712b (current) -> confirmed a revision landed, not a contradiction

whether infra retry could swallow real revise/deny -> no: retry wraps only _run_cursor_sdk exceptions/timeouts, valid outcomes go to fidelity/contract evaluation

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Public boundaries named in the plan all exist in current source.
Forbidden outcomes for P1/P2/P3/P5 are covered by real deterministic tests.
Detached submit retry-policy threading is implemented but untested.
Missing-module no-retry behavior is specified but untested.
Two plan-named tests do not exist under their stated names.

criteria: All named public boundaries exist in source (verified)
Forbidden outcomes for revise-blocks and missing!=accept are tested (verified)
Detached submit and missing-module acceptance criteria lack tests (verified by grep)
Tests not executed in this gate (unverified pass/fail)
confidence.rationale: Boundaries and RED premises verified directly against source; safety-critical forbidden outcomes confirmed covered. Gaps are real and map to explicit unchecked acceptance criteria. Did not execute the suite, and the original RED premise cannot be re-verified now that implementation has landed.

payload.content: Test plan is well-structured with valid public boundaries and full coverage of safety-critical forbidden outcomes (P1/P2/P3/P5). REVISE: detached submit (P4 boundary submit_dual_agent_workflow_job, Issue 2 acceptance) and missing-module no-retry (Issue 1 acceptance + PRD decision) have no tests, and two plan-named tests do not exist under their stated names (coverage folded into other tests). Implementation already landed in working tree.

assumptions_to_verify: The detached job payload retry policy is genuinely untested (no test under a different name asserts it)
Missing-module classification has no retry-loop guard test anywhere in the suite
contradictions_checked: Plan claims P4 coverage via test_workflow_passes_reviewer_infra_retry_policy_to_cursor_request, but P4 also names submit_dual_agent_workflow_job which that test does not exercise — contradiction confirmed
Plan traceability lists two tests by names that do not exist in the test files — confirmed via grep
missing_evidence: Full pytest run output (passed/failed) for the named regression commands

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

claims: The TDD plan covers P1-P5 with public-boundary RED tests.
Strict gate semantics (revise/deny blocks, contract retry separate, missing verdict non-accepting) are addressed by the plan.

criteria: 6 plan-named tests located by grep: 4 present, 2 absent under exact names
Issue 1 acceptance #4 and PRD 'Do not retry missing modules' have no corresponding test
P4 boundary submit_dual_agent_workflow_job not exercised for reviewer_infra_retry_*
test_status unknown (suite not executed in this gate)
evidence: test_workflow_kwargs_from_payload_preserves_reviewer_infra_retry_fields (named but assertions folded into test_workflow_kwargs_from_payload_preserves_dynamic_workflow_preview_fields:273, not standalone)
test_cursor_revise_still_blocks_with_retry_policy_enabled (NAMED IN PLAN BUT ABSENT; nearest is test_cursor_sdk_fallback_revise_still_blocks:805 at a different boundary)
confidence.rationale: Coverage gaps verified directly by source grep and reading acceptance criteria in issues.md/prd.md; high confidence the named tests are absent and the criteria are explicit. Lower than 0.9 because I did not run the suite, and the kwargs-preserve assertions do exist (folded into an existing test), so gap #attribution for P4 inline is partly mitigated.

payload.content: TDD plan is well-structured with public-boundary RED tests and P1-P5 traceability, and the implemented suite exceeds the named plan. However, three explicit acceptance criteria lack RED coverage: missing-module no-retry (Issue 1 #4 / PRD forbidden behavior), detached submit_dual_agent_workflow_job retry-policy round-trip (P4 boundary, Issue 2 #4), and the P5 named workflow-boundary revise-with-retry test does not exist (only an invoke-boundary fallback variant). Fallback-after-exhaustion ordering is also only asserted negatively. Decision: revise.

assumptions_to_verify: The folded kwargs-preserve assertions (driver:292-309) satisfy P4 inline-CLI intent
reviewer_infra_retry_limit=0 tests adequately exercise the no-retry classification path for modules (they do not — they bypass the loop)
Existing pre-fix regression tests still pass with retry policy enabled
contradictions_checked: Plan claims 6 named tests; grep confirms only 4 present under exact names
Plan P5 boundary is run_dual_agent_workflow but the only revise-still-blocks test is at the invoke_cursor_agent boundary
PRD P4 lists submit_dual_agent_workflow_job as a boundary; plan names no detached-submit test
missing_evidence: Full pytest run result for tests/test_cursor_agent.py and tests/test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py
Whether test_submit_workflow_job_payload_round_trips_agentic_policy_fields also asserts reviewer_infra_retry_* fields
Whether any test asserts fallback is invoked exactly once strictly after infra retries are exhausted
critical_review.strongest_objection: An explicit PRD forbidden behavior — never retry ModuleNotFoundError — has executable code inside the retry loop (cursor_agent.py:400) but zero RED test, so a regression that loops on a missing module would pass the gate undetected.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Adding (a) a RED test proving ModuleNotFoundError is classified without consuming retry attempts, (b) a detached submit_dual_agent_workflow_job round-trip test for reviewer_infra_retry_*, and (c) a run_dual_agent_workflow revise-with-retry-enabled regression matching the P5 named test, plus a green full-suite run, would move me to accept.

outcome.summary: TDD plan is well-structured with public-boundary RED tests and P1-P5 traceability, and the implemented suite exceeds the named plan. However, three explicit acceptance criteria lack RED coverage: missing-module no-retry (Issue 1 #4 / PRD forbidden behavior), detached submit_dual_agent_workflow_job retry-policy round-trip (P4 boundary, Issue 2 #4), and the P5 named workflow-boundary revise-with-retry test does not exist (only an invoke-boundary fallback variant). Fallback-after-exhaustion ordering is also only asserted negatively. Decision: revise.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: All public boundaries named in the plan exist in current source.
Each strict-semantic forbidden outcome has at least one covering test.
Plan fixtures are deterministic (fake _run_cursor_sdk, injected backoff sleep).

criteria: All PRD promises mapped to public-boundary RED tests
Forbidden outcomes protecting gate integrity each tested
confidence.rationale: Plan verified against PRD/issues and live source symbols; all strict semantics covered. Confidence held below 0.9 because two stated new behaviors (timeout retry, configured backoff) lack explicit assertions and I did not execute the tests.

payload.content: TDD plan reviewed against PRD/issues and live source. All five PRD promises (P1-P5) map to public-boundary RED tests on real symbols (verified via grep). Strict gate semantics each have a covering test: real revise/deny blocks, contract retry stays separate, missing verdict never accepts, fallback runs only after infra-retry exhaustion. Two additive coverage gaps noted (no explicit timeout-retry test; no backoff-sleep assertion). ACCEPT.

assumptions_to_verify: Live tdd.md matches handoff sha256 d4207ef3 (read live file; assumed no drift)
Exhausted-retry workflow test actually asserts diagnostics persistence and reviewer_verdict_counted_as_accept=false as described
contradictions_checked: Plan claims contract retry is separate from infra retry — confirmed by dedicated test + missing-module test
Plan claims fallback runs only after exhaustion — confirmed by P1 success test (fallback not called) and exhausted-degraded test
Checked grill Finding 2 deferral of timeout retry is a documented tradeoff, not silent omission
missing_evidence: A RED test asserting injected backoff sleep was called with reviewer_infra_retry_backoff_s
A deterministic faked-timeout test proving timeout enters the infra-retry loop
Live test run results (test_status unknown)
critical_review.strongest_objection: The plan asserts retry diagnostics and exhaustion but never explicitly verifies the backoff sleep was applied with the configured value, nor that a watchdog timeout (a PRD-stated retryable case) is actually retried rather than just classified.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If any strict-semantic forbidden outcome (revise blocks, missing verdict never accepts, contract/infra separation, fallback-after-exhaustion) lacked a covering test, or if a named public boundary did not exist in source, I would move to REVISE/DENY.

payload.content:
Independently review the tdd_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Plan correctly identifies all six work-surface files
Plan preserves revise/deny blocking (P5), separate contract vs infra budgets (P3), non-accepting missing verdicts (P2/P5), and fallback-only-after-exhaustion (P1)
Implementation matching the plan already exists in the working tree, confirming feasibility
Hash equality of on-disk plan to handoff (3fc2d73...) not cryptographically verified due to blocked sandbox bash

criteria: All six plan-targeted files exist and match work surface
All named public boundaries and retry fields verified via grep
Four strict-semantics requirements each mapped to a guardrail and a named test
evidence: tests/test_cursor_agent.py (planned, not run at this gate)
tests/test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py (planned, not run at this gate)
confidence.rationale: Plan is coherent, complete, and verifiably grounded in existing source boundaries with the exact field names and defaults; all intent requirements are guarded and traced. Confidence held below 0.95 because the on-di[REDACTED_API_KEY] hash equality could not be cryptographically confirmed (sandbox blocked) and tests are not run at this gate.

payload.content: Implementation plan is complete, internally consistent with PRD/issues/TDD, and grounded in real source boundaries (all named symbols and the reviewer_infra_retry fields exist). All four strict gate-semantics requirements from the intent are explicitly guarded and traced to named tests. Accept and advance to execution.

assumptions_to_verify: On-disk plan hash equals handoff packet hash
Idempotent job-token derivation change from new route fields is acceptable as a different logical request (plan asserts this; validate at execution/outcome gate)
contradictions_checked: Whether plan references fictional symbols — none; all boundaries verified in source
Whether guardrails contradict intent's strict semantics — they match all four
Whether contract retry and infra retry are conflated — plan keeps them separate (P3) and config defines distinct budgets
missing_evidence: Cryptographic confirmation that on-disk implementation-plan.md sha256 equals handoff hash 3fc2d731... (bash approval blocked)
Explicit dependency-wave ordering (currently only implicit via slice priorities P0/P0/P1)
Test execution results (not required at implementation_plan gate)
critical_review.strongest_objection: The implementation already appears landed in the working tree, so this implementation_plan gate may be reviewing a plan whose execution already occurred, risking post-hoc rationalization rather than forward planning.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A guardrail or traceability gap that left any one of the four strict-semantics requirements unmapped to a test, or a named public boundary in the plan that does not exist in source.

evidence_refs.ref: tests/test_cursor_agent.py (planned, not run at this gate)
tests/test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py (planned, not run at this gate)

Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.

Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Plan diagnoses a real defect (transient SDK exception immediately classified unavailable)
All named public boundaries exist in current source
Plan traces every PRD promise P1-P5 to specific tests and resolves grill findings 1-4
Plan structurally preserves contract-retry/infra-retry separation and missing-module non-retry

criteria: Named public boundaries exist in source (verified)
PRD promises map 1:1 to named tests (verified)
Strict-semantics guardrails structurally present (verified by inspection)
Tests executed and passing (NOT verified this gate)
confidence.rationale: Plan is fully grounded: every named symbol, config default, and the central retry helper verified present in source, and traceability to tests is complete. Held below 0.95 because no tests were executed in this gate; runtime confirmation of P5 AND-gate semantics and exhaustion-non-accepting behavior is deferred to the execution/outcome gate.

payload.content: Implementation plan for bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries is sound, grounded in real source boundaries, fully traces P1-P5 to named tests, and preserves strict AND-gate semantics (real revise/deny blocks, contract retry separate, missing module off retry budget, exhaustion non-accepting). Accept the plan; runtime test verification deferred to execution/outcome gate.

assumptions_to_verify: Infra-retry helper does not retry CursorSdkTimeout in a way that duplicates slow work beyond bounded limit
Route metadata records retry policy for audit (P4) as planned
Backoff sleep is bounded and configurable as defaults (2 / 1.0s) suggest
contradictions_checked: Plan-named symbols vs source (consistent)
Diagnosis vs actual current behavior at cursor_agent.py:218-226 (consistent)
Contract-retry vs infra-retry separation: outer loop 174-266 vs helper 381 (consistent)
Missing-module guardrail vs code path 208-217 (consistent)
missing_evidence: pytest run output for tests/test_cursor_agent.py and the five workflow-driver tests
runtime confirmation that reviewer_verdict_counted_as_accept stays false on exhausted infra retry
confirmation _run_cursor_sdk_with_infra_retries retries only infra exceptions, never typed verdicts
critical_review.strongest_objection: Tests were not run during this plan gate, so I cannot confirm the implemented retry loop actually keeps a real Cursor revise/deny blocking (P5) or that exhausted retries stay non-accepting at runtime; inspection of structure supports it but is not execution evidence.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the implemented loop retries on a typed revise/deny, or that exhaustion converts a missing verdict into accept, or that any named boundary/test is absent when the suite runs.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: P1 transient SDK exception retried before fallback
P2 exhausted retries yield typed recoverable reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable with attempt history
P3 contract retries and missing-module path do not consume infra retry budget
P4 retry policy threaded through config, inline+detached workflow, MCP, and CLI
P5 valid Cursor revise/deny still blocks; missing verdict never counts as accept

criteria: Diff present in working tree and inspected
Each PRD promise traced to specific code
New tests assert correct semantics and map to promises
Residual: live test execution not performed
confidence.rationale: Implementation is complete and correct under line-level static traceability across all five PRD promises, with tests that map precisely to each. Confidence is capped below 0.95 because pytest was not executed live, leaving runtime/import/fixture failures as the only unverified failure mode.

payload.content: Bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retry with backoff and diagnostics implemented in invoke_cursor_agent and threaded through config, inline+detached workflow routes, MCP signatures, and CLI keys. payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Bounded infra retries run before reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable classification
Real Cursor revise/deny still blocks via unchanged success-path verdict handling
Contract retry budget is separate from infra retry budget
Missing/exhausted Cursor verdicts never count as accept
Reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after infra retries exhausted

criteria: tests present for each acceptance criterion across 3 slices
strict gate semantics preserved by construction
test execution pass/fail confirmed (NOT met this session)
confidence.rationale: Static evidence is strong and complete: the diff implements every requirement correctly and tests exist for every acceptance criterion. Confidence is held below 0.9 solely because the test suite could not be executed in-session (permission-gated), leaving actual pass/fail unverified.

Independently review the outcome_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.

Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Contract corrective retry remains separate from infra retry budget
Cursor revise/deny verdicts still block the workflow
Missing/exhausted Cursor verdicts proceed degraded and never count as accept
Retry policy threaded through Config defaults, CLI payload, inline workflow, and detached job payload, and surfaced in workflow_route metadata

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Fix the Cursor reviewer reliability failure diagnosed on June 1, 2026. Add bounded Cursor SDK infrastructure retries with backoff and diagnostics before the workflow classifies Cursor as reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable. Preserve strict gate semantics: real Cursor revise/deny still blocks, contract retry remains separate, missing Cursor verdicts never count as accept, and existing reviewer-unavailable recovery/fallback runs only after retries are exhausted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

claims: contract retry loop is outer/separate from infra retry (cursor_agent.py:174 vs :189)
exhaustion returns typed reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable with attempt diagnostics (cursor_agent.py:444)
fallback runs only after infra retries exhausted (cursor_agent.py:190-195)
both test suites contain real assertions (183+226 insertions in diff)

criteria: diff matches handoff snapshot exactly (579 ins/6 files)
all 6 intent requirements located at concrete line numbers
contract retry confirmed as separate outer budget from infra retry
exhaustion path yields typed reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable not accept
gap: no live green test run obtainable in non-interactive gate
confidence.rationale: Implementation verified correct and complete on direct source inspection across 4 rounds with zero drift; tests are present and substantial (183+226 ins). Confidence capped below 0.9 because no observed green pytest run could be produced — execution is blocked by operator-side Bash approval, leaving runtime correctness unconfirmed by live evidence.

payload.content: All 6 intent requirements verified in current working tree via full source inspection of supervisor/cursor_agent.py; diff matches handoff exactly (579 ins, 6 files). Sole missing evidence is an observed green pytest run, which is blocked by an operator-side Bash-approval gap (4th consecutive round). Decision: ACCEPT on inspection evidence; escalate binary operator choice for live-test confirmation rather than issue a no-op REVISE.
